title: Aureline shell-level interaction-safety contract
description: Boundary schema for the interaction_safety_packet_record, the preview_apply_revert_record, the batch_scope_record, the permission_prompt_record, the copy_export_representation_record, the focus_return_record, the responsive_fallback_record, and the interaction_safety_audit_event_record frozen in docs/ux/shell_interaction_safety_contract.md. Every consequence-bearing interaction on the editor, terminal, review / diff, palette / search, install / update / attach, AI apply, collaboration, provider-bearing, docs / help / service-health, and support-export surfaces emits exactly one interaction_safety_packet_record. Raw bodies, raw paths, raw URLs, raw prompt text, and raw credential material never cross the RPC boundary; this schema carries ids and typed vocabulary only. The eventual shell / interaction-safety crate's Rust types are the schema of record; this file is the cross-tool boundary every non-owning surface reads. Adding a new authority class, consequence class, high-risk preview class, phase, revert class, permission grant scope, authority-renewal trigger, representation class, batch scope class, focus-return state, responsive-fallback mode, required-visible-field class, audit-event id, or denial reason is additive-minor and requires an interaction_safety_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

interaction_safety_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this interaction-safety vocabulary export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. Every record carries exactly one kind.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles / claim manifests. Raw bodies, raw paths, raw URLs, raw prompt text, and raw credential material never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

surface_class.description: Frozen surface-class vocabulary naming which protected shell surface emitted the interaction-safety record.

authority_class.description: Frozen authority-class vocabulary. Names the initiator of the consequence-bearing action, not the target. A surface that cannot resolve its authority class MUST deny with authority_class_unresolved rather than defaulting to user_initiated_local.

consequence_class.description: Frozen four-class destruction / mutation vocabulary re-exported from UI/UX Spec §9.4 verbatim. external_shared and irreversible_high_blast MUST focus the safest action by default and MUST NOT occupy the default Enter path unless the user is already inside an explicit destructive review. A surface MAY escalate a consequence class; it MAY NOT silently narrow.

high_risk_preview_class.description: Frozen typed preview classes that force the safe-preview posture (sanitised / sandboxed / raw-inspection / metadata-only) before bytes render or execute. A tooltip-only or hover-only disclosure is non-conforming; disclosures render on the primary surface.

preview_apply_revert_phase.description: Frozen preview / apply / revert phase vocabulary. Apply MAY NOT silently widen or materially change scope after preview. If the basis drifts, the surface MUST invalidate the preview, emit interaction_safety_apply_basis_drifted, and reopen review.

revert_class.description: Frozen revert-class vocabulary. evidence_only_no_rerun is the canonical claim for restored terminals, debug sessions, notebooks, API requests, and remote shells. no_recovery_available is permitted only on irreversible_high_blast consequence classes.

permission_grant_scope.description: Frozen permission grant-scope vocabulary re-exported from UI/UX Spec §9.6. Remembered decisions are never indefinite by default for destructive, networked, provider-backed, or secret-bearing capabilities. policy_managed grants are reviewable but not silently editable by the end user.

authority_renewal_trigger.description: Frozen authority-renewal triggers. A grant never silently crosses a target / route / actor / policy-source / epoch / trust / scope / expiry / revocation / basis / authority-class boundary. Any renewal trigger reopens the prompt with the delta (not the full envelope).

representation_class.description: Frozen representation-class vocabulary for copy / export. raw preserves exact bytes; rendered reflects the current rendered representation; escaped preserves source representation with metacharacters rendered safe; sanitized removes active / scriptable content; sandboxed renders within a safe boundary; generated names model-produced content (MUST carry citation_anchor_refs when quoting authoritative material); blocked_metadata_only withholds the raw body entirely.

batch_scope_class.description: Frozen batch-scope vocabulary re-exported from UI/UX Spec §9.12 and Appendix EO.3. Select all MUST say whether it means visible rows, loaded rows, or all matching items; query_snapshot_stale marks a selection built against a drifted query basis.

batch_member_status.description: Frozen batch-member-status vocabulary. Destructive batch previews MUST summarise every status present before commit.

focus_return_state.description: Frozen focus-return vocabulary re-exported from UI/UX Spec §19.4 and Appendix EL.2. Focus MUST return to the invoking control or its row / card on modal confirm / cancel; a surface that cannot locate a safe ancestor MUST render a returned_placeholder_announced placeholder whose announcement explains why focus could not return. Silent focus loss is non-conforming.

responsive_fallback_mode.description: Frozen responsive-fallback vocabulary re-exported from UI/UX Spec §6.1–§6.9 and Appendix EP. A responsive fallback MAY collapse secondary chrome; it MUST NOT hide any field from the required_visible_field_class set applicable to the current consequence class.

shell_zone.description: Frozen shell-zone vocabulary re-exported from UI/UX Spec Appendix EP.1.

required_visible_field_class.description: Frozen required-visible-field vocabulary. Fields that MUST remain visible at the same time as the confirm action on every external_shared and irreversible_high_blast consequence class, and on every high-risk preview class, even under the most aggressive responsive fallback.

validation_outcome.description: Frozen validation-outcome vocabulary from UI/UX Spec §9.5. passed MUST NOT be rendered while validation_plan_ref is null.

partial_outcome.description: Frozen partial-outcome vocabulary. Partial apply, partial validation, and partial revert are first-class outcomes with their own user-visible states, not footnotes inside generic Failed banners.

permission_decision_outcome.description: Frozen permission-decision vocabulary. Null until the user decides.

client_scope.description: Re-exported from schemas/governance/capability_lifecycle.schema.json without modification (ADR-0011). A row whose client_scope excludes the rendering surface MUST NOT render as available there.

redaction_class.description: Re-exported from schemas/governance/capability_lifecycle.schema.json without modification (ADR-0011). The broker-owned redaction pass (ADR-0007) runs before bytes reach any persistent or exportable sink.

audit_event_id.description: Frozen audit-event ids on the interaction_safety audit stream. Events MUST NOT carry raw bodies, raw paths, raw URLs, raw prompt text, or raw credential material.

denial_reason.description: Frozen denial-reason vocabulary. Denials fail closed; silent downgrade to a best-effort apply is forbidden.

trust_state.description: ADR-0001 workspace-trust state at the moment the record was minted.

execution_context_id.description: ADR-0009 execution-context id when the record was minted inside a live execution. Null when the record was minted outside an execution context.

policy_context.description: Policy context the record was minted under. Re-validated on every render.

actor_identity_ref.description: Opaque reference to the actor of record. Raw identity bodies never cross the boundary.

target_identity_ref.description: Opaque reference to the target of the interaction. Raw paths, raw URLs, raw handles, raw prompt text never cross the boundary.

high_risk_preview_classes.description: Every high-risk preview class that applies to this interaction. MAY be empty for reversible_local non-high-risk actions.

batch_scope_ref.description: Opaque reference to a batch_scope_record. Non-null when the interaction operates on more than one target.

preview_packet_ref.description: Opaque reference to the preview_apply_revert_record the apply phase is approved against. Required (non-null) when preview_apply_revert_phase = apply.

permission_prompt_ref.description: Opaque reference to the permission_prompt_record the interaction commits against. Non-null when a grant was used.

representation_class.description: Non-null whenever bytes leave the product on copy / export.

citation_anchor_refs.description: Anchor ids from schemas/docs/help_status_badge.schema.json that back the row's truth claim. Required (non-empty) when representation_class = generated and the row quotes authoritative material, or when surface_class = docs_help_service_health_canvas.

visible_fields_at_commit.description: The required-visible-field classes that were on screen at commit. MUST include every field the contract requires for the current consequence class. A surface whose responsive fallback hid any required field MUST deny with chrome_hid_required_field rather than emit this packet.

focus_return_ref.description: Opaque reference to the focus_return_record for this interaction. Non-null whenever the interaction closed a surface.

copy_export_refs.description: Opaque references to copy_export_representation_record entries produced by this interaction.

authority_renewal_triggers_observed.description: Every authority-renewal trigger the surface observed between the last grant and this packet. Non-empty whenever the packet represents a renewal / reissue.

basis_snapshot_ref.description: Opaque reference to the preview basis snapshot (query snapshot, repo revision, branch anchor, etc.) the interaction committed against.

running_build_identity_ref.description: Opaque pin to the running build's exact-build identity (D-0011).

allOf.description: preview_packet_ref MUST be non-null when preview_apply_revert_phase = apply.
citation_anchor_refs MUST be non-empty when representation_class = generated.

batch_scope_ref MUST be non-null whenever the interaction operates on more than one target. A surface MAY emit a batch_scope_record for a single target; this constraint is additive-optional and enforced at emission time rather than structurally here.
visible_fields_at_commit MUST include consequence_class_label, target_identity, and recovery_class_label at a minimum.

interaction_safety_packet_record.description: Per-interaction safety envelope. Raw bodies, raw paths, raw URLs, raw prompt text, and raw credential material never appear on this record; only ids and typed vocabulary cross the RPC boundary.

approval_requirements_ref.description: Opaque references to approval / step-up / policy-ack requirements that must be satisfied before this phase can proceed. Empty when no step-up is required.

validation_plan_ref.description: Opaque reference to the validation plan that accompanies this preview / apply. Required (non-null) on phase = preview and phase = apply; MAY be null on phase = propose.

allOf.description: validation_plan_ref MUST be non-null when phase = apply.
validation_outcome = passed MUST NOT be rendered while validation_plan_ref is null.

preview_apply_revert_record.description: Per-phase record with the shared lineage every phase quotes. Supports mechanical parity audit of propose / preview / apply / validate / keep_or_revert across surfaces.

query_basis_ref.description: Opaque reference to the query basis the selection was built from. Required (non-null) when batch_scope_class is all_matching_query or query_snapshot_stale.

member_summary.description: Typed membership summary. Destructive batch previews MUST summarise included, excluded, blocked, hidden, and query-derived members before commit.

stale_query_marker.description: True when the selection was built against a query snapshot that has since drifted. A surface that runs a destructive or broad action against a stale selection without rendering this marker is non-conforming.

allOf.description: query_basis_ref MUST be non-null when batch_scope_class is all_matching_query or query_snapshot_stale.

batch_scope_record.description: Typed batch-scope record for every interaction whose target set is more than one.

requester_identity_ref.description: Opaque reference to the actor requesting the grant (user, extension, AI tool, automation recipe, remote collaborator, connected provider, admin policy). Raw identity bodies never cross.

requested_capability_class.description: Typed capability-class name naming the precise boundary the grant covers (e.g. 'browser_handoff', 'extension_effective_permission', 'remote_attach').

denial_fallback_label.description: Short productive-fallback text rendered when the user denies (e.g. 'Continue with local-only review'). A dead-end refusal without a typed fallback is non-conforming when safe work is still possible.

revocation_path_label.description: Short inspector-path text rendered on the prompt (e.g. 'Settings › Trust › Active grants').

delta_summary_label.description: Short summary naming only the delta the prompt widens beyond the prior grant. Restating the full envelope as if everything were new is non-conforming.

authority_renewal_triggers.description: Every authority-renewal trigger the surface observed. Non-empty when the prompt is a renewal.

permission_prompt_record.description: One record per prompt open. Remembered decisions are never indefinite by default for destructive, networked, provider-backed, or secret-bearing capabilities.

citation_anchor_refs.description: Anchor ids from schemas/docs/help_status_badge.schema.json that back the bytes leaving the product. Required (non-empty) when representation_class = generated and the bytes quote authoritative material, and when source_surface_class = docs_help_service_health_canvas.

allOf.description: citation_anchor_refs MUST be non-empty when representation_class = generated.
citation_anchor_refs MUST be non-empty when source_surface_class = docs_help_service_health_canvas.

copy_export_representation_record.description: Emitted whenever bytes leave the product on copy / export. Representation labels survive screenshots, support exports, copied evidence, and review handoff artifacts.

return_target_identity_ref.description: Nullable when focus_return_state is focus_loss_denied or focus_not_applicable_non_interactive.

announcement_label.description: Required (non-null) on returned_placeholder_announced and focus_loss_denied. Explains why focus could not return to the missing surface.

allOf.description: announcement_label MUST be non-null when focus_return_state is returned_placeholder_announced or focus_loss_denied.

focus_return_record.description: Emitted on every close / dismiss / commit that closes a surface. Silent focus loss is non-conforming.

collapsed_chrome_items_ref.description: Opaque references to chrome items the surface collapsed (secondary nav rows, secondary detail panes, toolbars, etc.).

dropped_fields_if_any.description: Required-visible-field classes that were dropped under this fallback. MUST be empty for external_shared and irreversible_high_blast consequence classes; a non-empty array at commit denies the interaction with chrome_hid_required_field.

responsive_fallback_record.description: Emitted on every responsive-fallback engagement on a protected surface.

denial_reason.description: Required (non-null) for interaction_safety_denial_emitted and for any drift / disclosure event whose outcome was a denial.

allOf.description: denial_reason MUST be non-null for interaction_safety_denial_emitted events.

interaction_safety_audit_event_record.description: Audit event emitted on the interaction_safety audit stream. Events MUST NOT carry raw bodies, raw paths, raw URLs, raw prompt text, or raw credential material.
